laws.title: Public Law 1-2 | United States Bar Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology leaves the bar-exam rule current; the act is substantive and still needs a real operative Title 28 anchor.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Place the Attorney General control rule and bar-exam definition in operative Title 28 text, not as a Title 3 history note.
source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-001-002/law.txt states the Attorney General control rule and the bar-exam definition.
audit/primary/batch-10-01.json treats the act as operative-text-required with a Title 28 target.
usc/usc03.xml carries rp-pl001002-codification, while origin/main lacks the same note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch keeps the act as a note in usc/usc03.xml; origin/main does not carry that same implementation, and neither branch location is the proper operative Title 28 anchor.
laws.final_recommended_action: Move the rule to an operative Title 28 anchor and remove the mislocated note and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 1-3 | Closing Cabinet Doors Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology keeps the act current, but its enactment is structural and belongs in a concise history note rather than direct section strikes.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Keep a concise structural history note at Title 5 and record the department closures, VA transfer, and blanket repeal history without inventing section-level strikes.
source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-001-003/law.txt shows the department-closure and blanket-repeal language.
audit/primary/batch-10-01.json classifies the law as note-only with a blanket repeal component.
usc/usc05.xml carries rp-pl001003-codification, while origin/main lacks the same note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch note in usc/usc05.xml still contains the long quoted-content dump and archive link; origin/main lacks that same branch note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep only the structural history note and strip the dump and archive link from the branch note.

laws.title: Public Law 2-5 | Judicial Efficiency Act (Keywords: Maximum, Minimum, Sentence, Sentencing)
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology and later repeal history make this a repealed law that should remain history only.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Retain only a concise historical note for PL 2-5; do not restore the superseded sentencing ceilings.
source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-002-005/law.txt is the original sentencing-reform text, but later law PL 19-149 replaced it.
audit/primary/batch-10-01.json marks PL 2-5 as fully repealed by PL 19-149.
usc/usc18.xml carries rp-pl002005-codification, while origin/main lacks that same branch note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch keeps a repeal-history note in usc/usc18.xml with a long dump and archive link; origin/main does not show the same branch note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep only a concise repeal-history note and remove the dump and archive link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-8 | National Security Act of 2021
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology leaves this act repealed in full and replaced by later national-security legislation.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Retain only a concise historical note stating enactment and repeal in favor of later national-security legislation.

audit/primary/batch-10-01.json marks the act as fully repealed and replaced in revised form.
usc/usc50.xml and origin/main both carry a Title 50 history note, so the live issue is cleanup rather than a new insertion.

laws.final_recommended_action: Keep the history-note treatment, trim the quoted-content dump, and preserve the repeal/replacement cross-reference.

laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology leaves the charter current as a local-law note, not as positive-law Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 2-10 | Amendment to the District of Columbia City Charter
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology leaves this local amendment current, and the charter text must be updated rather than only noted.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Amend the charter's mayoral term to 90 days and council term to 45 days, and apply the stated partial repeal and amendment to Article III 3.02(a) and Article II 2.01(A(a)(i)).
source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-002-010/law.txt sets the 90-day and 45-day charter changes.
audit/primary/batch-10-01.json classifies the act as a live local charter amendment that needs operative text handling.
usc/usc40.xml carries rp-pl002010-codification, while origin/main lacks the same branch note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch shows rp-pl002010-codification in usc/usc40.xml; origin/main lacks the same branch note, and the charter amendment is still note-only.
laws.final_recommended_action: Carry the term changes into the charter text or proper local-law apparatus and remove the archive link.

laws.title: Public Law 2-11 | Criminal Statute of Limitations Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology and repeal history show this statute is repealed, so it should stay as history only.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Keep only a historical note for the statute-of-limitations rules and preserve the repeal posture rather than reviving the 75-day or 150-day scheme.
source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-002-011/law.txt sets the 75-day general rule, the 150-day exceptions, and the accrual rule.
audit/primary/batch-10-02.json classifies the act as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.
usc/usc18.xml carries rp-pl002011-codification, while origin/main lacks the same branch note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch keeps a note-only placeholder in usc/usc18.xml; origin/main does not provide a better resolved section anchor either.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep only a historical note and ensure the repeal marker remains intact.

laws.title: Public Law 2-12 | Fee Prohibition Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is no longer operative because a later law expressly repeals it in full; only repeal history survives.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Keep only a historical note for the court-fee prohibition and record the later repeal by PL 4-30 section 7.
source_evidence: audit/primary/batch-10-02.json classifies PL-002-012 as fully-repealed and ties the repeal to PL 4-30 section 7.
usc/usc50.xml:1583 contains rp-pl002012-codification with a repealed or superseded history note.
origin/main has no matching PL-002-012 note in the live XML.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch preserves a history note in usc/usc50.xml, but the proper posture is repeal history only at the Title 28 anchor; origin/main does not carry the note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave PL-002-012 as repeal history only and ensure the repeal marker remains intact.
